edges.description: Loss of ATM redox and DSB-response functions drives oxidative stress and microglial activation in the cerebellum.
edges.source: ATM kinase deficiency and defective DNA damage signaling

edges.description: ATM is required for V(D)J recombination and class switch recombination, so its loss disrupts adaptive immunity.

edges.description: Loss of insulin-activated ATM disrupts the catabolic-anabolic glucose switch and produces intrinsic insulin resistance.

edges.description: Defective DSB repair and checkpoint signaling produce chromosomal instability and malignant transformation.
edges.source: ATM kinase deficiency and defective DNA damage signaling

edges.description: Loss of ATM-dependent DSB repair confers cellular hypersensitivity to ionizing radiation and radiomimetic agents.
edges.source: ATM kinase deficiency and defective DNA damage signaling

edges.description: Persistent elevation of serum AFP is a recognized laboratory hallmark of ATM deficiency.
edges.source: ATM kinase deficiency and defective DNA damage signaling

edges.description: Progressive loss of Purkinje and granule neurons drives the macroscopic cerebellar atrophy seen on imaging.
edges.source: Oxidative stress and microglia-driven cerebellar neurodegeneration

edges.description: Defective V(D)J/class switch recombination yields the clinical combined immunodeficiency phenotype.
edges.source: Combined immunodeficiency with lymphocyte depletion and dysfunction

edges.description: Impaired class switch recombination in B cells produces selective IgA deficiency in the majority of patients.
edges.source: Combined immunodeficiency with lymphocyte depletion and dysfunction

edges.description: Cell-intrinsic epithelial inflammasome and oxidative defects predispose the airway to recurrent pyogenic infection.
edges.source: Airway epithelial oxidative susceptibility and impaired innate immunity

edges.description: Repeated airway injury and infection drive airway remodeling and bronchiectasis.
edges.source: Airway epithelial oxidative susceptibility and impaired innate immunity

edges.description: Loss of insulin-activated ATM produces intrinsic insulin resistance and glucose intolerance.
edges.source: Defective insulin-ATM signaling and metabolic inflexibility

edges.description: Metabolic inflexibility and chronic insulin resistance contribute to progressive liver injury and fibrosis in AT.
edges.source: Defective insulin-ATM signaling and metabolic inflexibility

edges.description: Chromosomal instability at lymphoid antigen-receptor loci drives non-Hodgkin lymphoma, the predominant childhood malignancy in AT.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in the ATM gene causing ataxia-telangiectasia, including a duplication of 90 kb: Utility of targeted next-generation sequencing in detection of copy number variation.
evidence.snippet: Classical A-T is caused by biallelic variants on ATM (ataxia telangiectasia mutated) gene, leading to a loss of function of the protein kinase ATM, involved in DNA damage repair.

evidence.reference_title: Pre-emptive Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation in Ataxia Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: The incidence of cancer does not correlate with the type of ATM mutation, but rather with the extent of immunodeficiency, particularly profound IgA deficiency and a low number of B cells (1).

evidence.reference_title: ATM germ line pathogenic variants affect outcomes in children with ataxia-telangiectasia and hematological malignancies.
evidence.snippet: Four-year EFS was 39.4% (95% CI, 29-53.3) vs 78.7% (95% CI, 63.7-97.2), (P < .001), and TRM rates were 37.6% (95% CI, 26.4-48.7) vs 4.0% (95% CI, 0-11.8), (P = .017), for those with absent and residual ATM kinase activity, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: ATM germ line pathogenic variants affect outcomes in children with ataxia-telangiectasia and hematological malignancies.
evidence.snippet: Patients with A-T and leukemia/lymphoma may benefit from deescalated therapy for patients with absent ATM kinase activity and near-standard therapy regimens for those with residual kinase activity.

evidence.reference_title: ATM / ataxia telangiectasia (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: ATM | HGNC:795 | ataxia telangiectasia | MONDO:0008840 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: ATM loss of function impairs the cellular response to DNA double-strand breaks and checkpoint signaling, creating genomic instability and broad tissue vulnerability. In classical disease, biallelic ATM variants abolish or markedly reduce ATM kinase activity. ATM normally coordinates DSB repair through the MRN complex (MRE11-RAD50-NBN) and downstream effectors including p53 and CHK2.
nodes.id: ATM kinase deficiency and defective DNA damage signaling

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-telangiectasia: A review of clinical features and molecular pathology.
evidence.snippet: The principal role of nuclear ATM is the coordination of cellular signaling pathways in response to DNA double-strand breaks, oxidative stress, and cell cycle checkpoint.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in the ATM gene causing ataxia-telangiectasia, including a duplication of 90 kb: Utility of targeted next-generation sequencing in detection of copy number variation.
evidence.snippet: Classical A-T is caused by biallelic variants on ATM (ataxia telangiectasia mutated) gene, leading to a loss of function of the protein kinase ATM, involved in DNA damage repair.

nodes.description: AT respiratory disease is not explained solely by systemic immune defects. ATM-deficient airway epithelial cells show increased oxidative injury after pneumococcal challenge together with defective ASC-Caspase 1 inflammasome activation and altered cytokine output, providing a local epithelial mechanism for recurrent sinopulmonary disease.
nodes.id: Airway epithelial oxidative susceptibility and impaired innate immunity

evidence.reference_title: Increased susceptibility of airway epithelial cells from ataxia-telangiectasia to S. pneumoniae infection due to oxidative damage and impaired innate immunity.
evidence.snippet: These data suggest that the heightened susceptibility of these cells to S. pneumoniae infection is due to both increased oxidative damage and a defect in inflammasome activation, and has implications for lung disease in these patients.

evidence.reference_title: Increased susceptibility of airway epithelial cells from ataxia-telangiectasia to S. pneumoniae infection due to oxidative damage and impaired innate immunity.
evidence.snippet: Further investigation revealed that the ASC-Caspase 1 signalling pathway was defective in A-T airway epithelial cells.

nodes.description: Investigational metabolic therapy supplementing 2-oxoglutarate (the alpha-keto acid backbone of glutamine) to address the glutamine dependence seen in ATM-deficient cells. In an Atm-deficient mouse model, alpha- ketoglutarate alleviated glutamine dependence and attenuated Purkinje cell degeneration, supporting its preclinical rationale.

evidence.reference_title: Alpha-ketoglutarate mitigates insulin resistance and metabolic inflexibility in a mouse model of Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Supplementation with α-ketoglutarate, the α-keto acid backbone of glutamine, has demonstrated potentials in alleviating glutamine dependence and attenuating Purkinje cell degeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Increased susceptibility of airway epithelial cells from ataxia-telangiectasia to S. pneumoniae infection due to oxidative damage and impaired innate immunity.
evidence.snippet: Respiratory disease is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in patients with ataxia-telangiectasia (A-T) who are prone to recurrent sinopulmonary infections, bronchiectasis, pulmonary fibrosis, and pulmonary failure.

evidence.reference_title: Increased oxidative stress in ataxia telangiectasia evidenced by alterations in redox state of brains from Atm-deficient mice.
evidence.snippet: A-T patients display a pleiotropic phenotype and suffer primarily from progressive ataxia caused by degeneration of cerebellar Purkinje and granule neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: neurologically, dysarthria, oculomotor apraxia, extrapyramidal symptoms, axonal neuropathy, and cognitive impairment are common

evidence.reference_title: Immune profiling and functional analysis of NK and T cells in ataxia telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Ataxia telangiectasia (AT) is a rare autosomal-recessive disorder characterized by profound neurodegeneration, combined immunodeficiency, and an increased risk for malignant diseases.

nodes.description: ATM deficiency disrupts adaptive immune homeostasis through impaired V(D)J recombination and class switch recombination. This leads to B-cell lymphopenia, defective immunoglobulin class switching (IgA deficiency in ~61%, IgG deficiency in ~29%), T-cell depletion, and NK-cell functional abnormalities. IgA deficiency is a strong prognostic indicator of worse survival.
nodes.id: Combined immunodeficiency with lymphocyte depletion and dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-telangiectasia in Latin America: clinical features, immunodeficiency, and mortality in a multicenter study.
evidence.snippet: IgA deficiency was observed in 60.8% of patients and IgG deficiency in 28.6%. T- and B-lymphopenias were also present in most cases.

evidence.reference_title: Immune profiling and functional analysis of NK and T cells in ataxia telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: We observed reduced levels of peripheral blood CD3+CD8+ cytotoxic T cells, CD3+CD4+ T helper cells, and CD19+ B cells, whereas the amount of CD3--CD56+ NK cells and CD3+CD56+ NKT-like cells was similar compared with age-matched controls.

evidence.reference_title: Simple Measurement of IgA Predicts Immunity and Mortality in Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Overall survival of patients with IgA deficiency was significantly diminished.

evidence.reference_title: Class switch recombination process in ataxia telangiectasia patients with elevated serum levels of IgM.
evidence.snippet: Laboratory evaluations revealed defective CSR while none of the three AT patients without HIgM presentation had a defect in the CSR process.

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-telangiectasia in Latin America: clinical features, immunodeficiency, and mortality in a multicenter study.
evidence.snippet: IgA deficiency was observed in 60.8% of patients and IgG deficiency in 28.6%. T- and B-lymphopenias were also present in most cases.

evidence.reference_title: Simple Measurement of IgA Predicts Immunity and Mortality in Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: IgA, a simple surrogate marker, is indicating the poorest prognosis for classical A-T patients.

nodes.description: ATM is activated by insulin and acts as a critical regulator of metabolic flexibility, mediating the switch between catabolic and anabolic glucose fates by controlling PKM2 and HIF1alpha. In AT, loss of this insulin-ATM axis produces intrinsic insulin resistance and glucose intolerance, and cells become compensatorily dependent on glutamine for energy. Purkinje cells are unusually insulin-sensitive, providing a metabolic explanation for their disproportionate vulnerability in AT cerebellum and linking peripheral metabolic disease (insulin-resistant diabetes, hepatic metabolic injury) to neurodegenerative progression.
nodes.id: Defective insulin-ATM signaling and metabolic inflexibility

evidence.reference_title: Alpha-ketoglutarate mitigates insulin resistance and metabolic inflexibility in a mouse model of Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Here we show insulin-activated ATM is a critical mediator of this process, facilitating the swift transition between catabolic-and-anabolic fates of glucose by regulating the functional status of PKM2 and HIF1α.

evidence.reference_title: Alpha-ketoglutarate mitigates insulin resistance and metabolic inflexibility in a mouse model of Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: In Ataxia-Telangiectasia (A-T), these mechanisms are disrupted, resulting in intrinsic insulin resistance and glucose intolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Alpha-ketoglutarate mitigates insulin resistance and metabolic inflexibility in a mouse model of Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Cerebellar degeneration, a hallmark of A-T, is characterized by the pronounced vulnerability of Purkinje cells, attributed to their unexpected sensitivity to insulin.

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: neurologically, dysarthria, oculomotor apraxia, extrapyramidal symptoms, axonal neuropathy, and cognitive impairment are common

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in the ATM gene causing ataxia-telangiectasia, including a duplication of 90 kb: Utility of targeted next-generation sequencing in detection of copy number variation.
evidence.snippet: Ataxia-telangiectasia (A-T) is a rare autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by progressive cerebellar ataxia, ocular apraxia, immunodeficiency, telangiectasia, elevated serum α-fetoprotein concentration, radiosensitivity and cancer predisposition.

nodes.description: ATM deficiency produces chromosomal instability, defective cell-cycle checkpoints, and impaired apoptotic responses, driving malignant transformation particularly in lymphoid tissues. The cumulative incidence of cancer reaches 29% by age 35, with hematologic malignancies (especially non-Hodgkin lymphoma) predominating in childhood and solid tumors becoming more frequent in adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence and outcomes of cancer and treatment-associated toxicities for patients with ataxia telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Eighty-four (16.5%) of 508 individuals were diagnosed with a primary cancer, of whom 62 (74%) were hematologic in origin and 22 (26%) were solid-organ cancers.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence and outcomes of cancer and treatment-associated toxicities for patients with ataxia telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Non-Hodgkin lymphoma occurred most frequently (n = 39), whereas solid cancers disproportionately affected those 18 years and older (n = 22).

evidence.reference_title: Pre-emptive Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation in Ataxia Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Apart from a progressive neurodegenerative disorder, A-T leads to significantly increased susceptibility to malignancies.

nodes.description: Preemptive allogeneic HSCT can correct the immunodeficiency in AT and may prevent hematological malignancy in high-risk patients. Limited experience suggests acceptable transplant-related risk in selected patients with severe immunodeficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pre-emptive Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation in Ataxia Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: pre-emptive alloHSCT can correct immunodeficiency in A-T with an acceptable risk of transplant-related mortality, and might be an early treatment of choice in some A-T patients at high risk of hematological malignancy.

evidence.reference_title: Pre-emptive Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation in Ataxia Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, this treatment provided complete immunological reconstitution and remission of the patient's granulomas without any need for immunosuppression or immunoglobulin replacement.

evidence.reference_title: Hepatic fibrosis: a manifestation of the liver disease evolution in patients with Ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Significant hepatic fibrosis was observed in 5/25 (20%).

evidence.reference_title: Hepatic fibrosis: a manifestation of the liver disease evolution in patients with Ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: A non-invasive diagnosis of significant hepatic fibrosis was observed in 20% of A-T patients associated with changes in liver enzymes, ferritin, increased HOMA-AD, and the severity of ataxia in comparison with patients without hepatic fibrosis.

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-telangiectasia: A review of clinical features and molecular pathology.
evidence.snippet: A-T patients represent a broad range of clinical manifestations including progressive cerebellar ataxia, oculocutaneous telangiectasia, variable immunodeficiency, radiosensitivity, susceptibility to malignancies, and increased metabolic diseases.

evidence.reference_title: Dysfunction of cerebellar microglia in Ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Ataxia-telangiectasia (A-T) is a multisystem autosomal recessive disease caused by mutations in the ATM gene and characterized by cerebellar atrophy, progressive ataxia, immunodeficiency, male and female sterility, radiosensitivity, cancer predisposition, growth retardation, insulin-resistant diabetes, and premature aging.

evidence.reference_title: Alpha-ketoglutarate mitigates insulin resistance and metabolic inflexibility in a mouse model of Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: In Ataxia-Telangiectasia (A-T), these mechanisms are disrupted, resulting in intrinsic insulin resistance and glucose intolerance.

nodes.description: Investigational corticosteroid delivery system (EryDex) evaluated for neurologic symptoms in AT. The ATTeST phase 3 trial (NCT02770807, n=176) showed acceptable safety but did not meet the primary efficacy endpoint overall, though prespecified subgroup analysis suggested possible benefit in children aged 6-9 years.

evidence.reference_title: Safety and efficacy of intra-erythrocyte dexamethasone sodium phosphate in children with ataxia telangiectasia (ATTeST): a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial.
evidence.snippet: Although there were no safety concerns, the primary efficacy endpoint was not met, possibly related to delays in treatment reducing the number of participants who received treatment as outlined in the protocol, and potentially different treatment effects according to age.

evidence.reference_title: Safety and efficacy of intra-erythrocyte dexamethasone sodium phosphate in children with ataxia telangiectasia (ATTeST): a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial.
evidence.snippet: There were no reports of hyperglycaemia, hypertension, hirsutism, or Cushingoid appearance in any of the treatment groups, nor any treatment-related deaths.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence and outcomes of cancer and treatment-associated toxicities for patients with ataxia telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Non-Hodgkin lymphoma occurred most frequently (n = 39), whereas solid cancers disproportionately affected those 18 years and older (n = 22).

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in the ATM gene causing ataxia-telangiectasia, including a duplication of 90 kb: Utility of targeted next-generation sequencing in detection of copy number variation.
evidence.snippet: Ataxia-telangiectasia (A-T) is a rare autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by progressive cerebellar ataxia, ocular apraxia, immunodeficiency, telangiectasia, elevated serum α-fetoprotein concentration, radiosensitivity and cancer predisposition.

nodes.description: Cerebellar degeneration in AT is linked to persistent oxidative stress and inflammatory signaling. ATM deficiency increases reactive oxygen species in the cerebellum and is associated with activated microglia that deliver neurotoxic cytokine signals to Purkinje and granule neurons. ATM expression is enriched in microglia throughout cerebellar development and adulthood, and pseudotime analysis shows microglial activation precedes neuronal apoptosis.
nodes.id: Oxidative stress and microglia-driven cerebellar neurodegeneration

evidence.reference_title: Increased oxidative stress in ataxia telangiectasia evidenced by alterations in redox state of brains from Atm-deficient mice.
evidence.snippet: Our findings support the hypothesis that the absence of functional ATM results in oxidative stress, which may be an important cause of the degeneration of cerebellar neurons in A-T.

evidence.reference_title: Increased oxidative stress in ataxia telangiectasia evidenced by alterations in redox state of brains from Atm-deficient mice.
evidence.snippet: We found alterations in the levels of thiol-containing compounds in Atm (-/-) brains, as well as significant changes in the activities of thioredoxin, catalase, and manganese superoxide dismutase in Atm (-/-) cerebella.

evidence.reference_title: ATM-deficiency-induced microglial activation promotes neurodegeneration in ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Here, we find evidence of microglial inflammation in the cerebellum of patients with A-T using single-nucleus RNA sequencing.

evidence.reference_title: ATM-deficiency-induced microglial activation promotes neurodegeneration in ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: A-T microglia co-culture with either control or A-T iPSC-derived neurons was sufficient to induce cytotoxicity.

evidence.reference_title: ATM-deficiency-induced microglial activation promotes neurodegeneration in ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Pseudotime analysis revealed that activation of A-T microglia preceded upregulation of apoptosis-related genes in granule and Purkinje neurons and that microglia exhibited increased neurotoxic cytokine signaling to granule and Purkinje neurons in A-T.

evidence.reference_title: Dysfunction of cerebellar microglia in Ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Here we demonstrate that microglia derived from Atm-/- mouse cerebellum display accelerated cell migration and are severely impaired in phagocytosis, secretion of neurotrophic factors, and mitochondrial activity, suggestive of apoptotic processes.

evidence.reference_title: Dysfunction of cerebellar microglia in Ataxia-telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Interestingly, no microglial impairment was detected in Atm-deficient cerebral cortex, and Atm deficiency had less impact on astroglia than microglia.

nodes.description: Supportive rehabilitation focused on balance, strength, and functional mobility can improve participation and motor function in individual AT patients.

evidence.reference_title: Effectiveness of Physical Therapy on Ataxia-Telangiectasia: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Positive changes were observed in the TCMS, PBBS, GMFM, and motor performance, participation, and quality of life.

nodes.description: Investigational use of the thiazolidinedione PPAR-gamma agonist pioglitazone in AT, motivated by reported benefits on AT cell biology and on diabetic AT individuals. Targets the insulin resistance and metabolic inflexibility associated with ATM deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pioglitazone as a Possible Treatment for Ataxia-Telangiectasia.
evidence.snippet: Recently, pioglitazone, a thiazolidinedione class used to treat type 2 diabetes, has been demonstrated to exert beneficial effects on AT cells and on diabetic individuals with AT.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in the ATM gene causing ataxia-telangiectasia, including a duplication of 90 kb: Utility of targeted next-generation sequencing in detection of copy number variation.
evidence.snippet: Ataxia-telangiectasia (A-T) is a rare autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by progressive cerebellar ataxia, ocular apraxia, immunodeficiency, telangiectasia, elevated serum α-fetoprotein concentration, radiosensitivity and cancer predisposition.

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-telangiectasia in Latin America: clinical features, immunodeficiency, and mortality in a multicenter study.
evidence.snippet: Median (IQR) ages at symptom onset and diagnosis were 1.0 (1.0-2.0) and 5.0 (3.0-8.0) years, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Ataxia-telangiectasia in Latin America: clinical features, immunodeficiency, and mortality in a multicenter study.
evidence.snippet: Most patients presented recurrent airway infections, which was significantly associated with IgA deficiency.
